matrix.case: Multi-term query ranks candidates by term overlap, higher overlap first
matrix.expected: docs/beta/graph-database (2/2 terms) before docs/alpha/graph-theory (1/2) before docs/gamma/unrelated-topic (0/2)

matrix.case: Delegation still goes through btree.PrefixScan (not some other index)
matrix.test: TestSearchCandidates/RanksByTermOverlap (new) -- fixture only has a btree, no other store backing candidates
matrix.expected: candidates set == PrefixScan(query-prefix) filtered set, just reordered

matrix.case: Empty query is a ranking no-op, preserving shortlist()'s pool-retrieval contract
matrix.expected: resp order == raw btree.PrefixScan(store, root, "") order

matrix.case: MaxResults caps the RANKED (not raw) list -- top-K semantics
matrix.expected: single returned candidate is the highest-scoring match, not just any PrefixScan-order match

matrix.case: Existing SearchCandidates subtest (single-prefix match) still passes
matrix.expected: unchanged -- only one match for "topics/alpha/", ranking is irrelevant with a 1-element result set

matrix.case: max_results cap still returns exactly 1 result for a 2-match prefix
matrix.expected: unchanged -- count assertion only, no order assertion; both fixture paths score equally for query "topics/" so rank-then-cap is stable

matrix.case: No-match prefix still returns zero results
matrix.expected: unchanged -- ranking a zero-length slice is a no-op

matrix.case: Cross-process integration test's SearchCandidates subtest still passes
matrix.expected: unchanged -- test sorts both got/want path lists before comparing, so is order-independent

matrix.test: covered by existing TestRPCServerHandlers table-driven error cases (grep-confirmed during implementation) -- not re-tested in new file, no regression expected since that code path is untouched

matrix.case: nil btreeStore degraded mode still returns empty response, not an error
matrix.test: covered by existing tests -- untouched code path
